claim: QwQ 32B is a distilled dense model that matches or exceeds DeepSeekR1 (mixture-of-experts) on AIME 24 benchmark despite being 21x smaller in parameters
actionable: For local reasoning tasks, use QwQ 32B instead of attempting to run larger MoE models; it offers comparable performance at lower resource cost
source_title: Qwen QwQ 32B - The Best Local Reasoning Model?

claim: QwQ uses two-stage RL: outcome-based rewards (accuracy verification on math/code) followed by general capability training with reward models and rule-based verifiers
actionable: When training reasoning models, separate outcome-based RL (for verifiable tasks) from general RL; use verifiable rewards for scaling before human feedback
source_title: Qwen QwQ 32B - The Best Local Reasoning Model?

claim: Thinking tokens (chain-of-thought) in reasoning models help with planning and non-coding tasks, not just math/code; they improve general reasoning quality
actionable: Use reasoning models with extended thinking for complex planning and analysis tasks, not just coding and math
source_title: Qwen QwQ 32B - The Best Local Reasoning Model?

claim: QwQ 32B can be run locally with 4-bit quantization on consumer hardware (tested on Mac Mini); provides reasonable speed (3 tokens/sec on M1) for local inference
actionable: Deploy QwQ 32B locally with 4-bit quantization for privacy and offline operation; expect 2-4 tokens/sec depending on hardware
source_title: Qwen QwQ 32B - The Best Local Reasoning Model?
